# Zen BBQ 2.0

Zen BBQ (Build Bevels Quickly) is a professional toolset for Blender designed for creating, adjusting, visualizing, and baking non-destructive bevels. It leverages the Cycles Bevel Shader to achieve realistic rounded edges without complex topology, allowing for a fully non-destructive workflow where bevels are generated at the shader level and can be baked into production-ready Normal, AO, and Curvature maps.

## Metadata
- **Blender Version:** 4.2+ (installed as a local extension).
- **Global Hotkey:** `Ctrl + Shift + X` (Pie Menu).
- **UI Location:** N-Panel -> Zen BBQ tab.
- **Core Engine:** Cycles (required for procedural bevel preview and baking).

## Core Documentation
- [index.md](mkdocs/index.md): Introduction to core concepts, advantages of non-destructive beveling, and application areas.
- [installation.md](mkdocs/installation.md): Step-by-step instructions for installing the addon as a local Blender Extension.
- [quickstart.md](mkdocs/quickstart.md): Essential workflow steps for assigning, previewing, and baking your first bevels.
- [subpanel_bevels.md](mkdocs/subpanel_bevels.md): Detailed guide on managing bevel presets, color-coded overlays, and viewport display modes (Material, Shader, Image).
- [subpanel_bake.md](mkdocs/subpanel_bake.md): Comprehensive guide to the baking pipeline, including object monitoring, quality presets, and naming templates.
- [subpanel_tools.md](mkdocs/subpanel_tools.md): Overview of advanced utilities like Live Boolean, Explode System, and Vertex Color Masking.
- [piemenu.md](mkdocs/piemenu.md): Reference for fast viewport commands available via the `Ctrl+Shift+X` shortcut.
- [live_boolean.md](mkdocs/live_boolean.md): Deep dive into the automated workflow for beveling dynamic Boolean intersections.
- [subpanel_preferences.md](mkdocs/subpanel_preferences.md): Customization of hotkeys, UI styles, default materials, and panel layout management.
- [manual_normal_map_baking.md](mkdocs/manual_normal_map_baking.md): Tutorial for manually baking Zen BBQ bevels using native Cycles settings.
- [marmoset-integration.md](mkdocs/marmoset-integration.md): Workflow for transferring variable-width bevel data to Marmoset Toolbag using Vertex Color masks.
- [troubleshooting.md](mkdocs/troubleshooting.md): Solutions for common issues related to installation, viewport display, and baking artifacts.
- [subpanel_help.md](mkdocs/subpanel_help.md): Access to community Discord, official documentation, and interactive example scenes.

## Quick Reference

### Installation
- **Method 1 (Drag-and-Drop):** Drag `ZenBBQ_2_0.zip` directly into the Blender 3D Viewport and click **Install**.
- **Method 2 (Get Extensions):** Go to `Edit > Preferences > Get Extensions`, click the top-right arrow, select **Install from Disk...**, and choose the zip file.

### Assigning Bevels
- **N-Panel:** Select geometry, go to **Zen BBQ** tab, and click the **"●"** button next to a preset radius.
- **Pie Menu:** Press `Ctrl + Shift + X` and select a color-coded preset from the wheel.
- **Modes:** In **Edit Mode**, assignments apply to selected edges/vertices. In **Object Mode**, assignments apply to the entire object.
- **Visualization:** Toggle the **Highlight Bevels** icon (intersecting circles) to see color-coded bevel weights in the viewport.

### Managing Presets
- **Groups:** Use the Preset Groups dropdown to switch between units (mm, cm, m, in, ft).
- **Customization:** Click the **"+"** button to create a new group. Click any preset value to edit its radius or unit.
- **Resetting:** Go to **Preferences** and click **Reset Preferences** to restore factory defaults.

### Selection Tools
- **Smart Select:** In Edit Mode, select an edge and click the **Smart Select** icon (arrow with eye) to select all edges with the same radius.
- **Select by Preset:** Choose a preset in the list and click the **Select by Selected Preset** button to find all matching geometry.

### Baking Workflow
- **UV Check:** Ensure the object has a valid UV map without flipped islands or overlaps (checked automatically by Object Monitor).
- **Quality:** In the **Bake Panel**, select **Low**, **Med**, or **High** quality from the **Bake Render Presets**.
- **Start Bake:** Click the **Start Bake** (Play) icon. Monitor progress and errors in the **Object Monitor** log.
- **Preview:** Switch to **Image** mode in the Bevels panel to verify baked results directly on the model.
